conmon_message:0x0081.name: set_sample_rate
conmon_message:0x0081.note: Sets device sample rate. Unicast request to device port 8700. Fire-and-forget — no unicast reply. Confirmation arrives via multicast notification burst: 0x0100 (+47ms), 0x0086 transition marker (+810ms), sample_rate_announcement 0x0080 with new rate (+1.3s), 0x0082 (+1.6s), 0x0100 (+2s), 0x0120 (+2.7s), 0x0102 (+2.8s).

history.previous_note: Sets device sample rate via unicast on port 8700. Target rate as uint32_be at offset 36.

Sets device sample rate. Unicast request to device port 8700. Fire-and-forget — no unicast reply. Confirmation arrives via multicast notification burst: 0x0100 (+47ms), 0x0086 transition marker (+810ms), sample_rate_announcement 0x0080 with new rate (+1.3s), 0x0082 (+1.6s), 0x0100 (+2s), 0x0120 (+2.7s), 0x0102 (+2.8s). Dante Controller sends from port 8700 (src=dst=8700). netaudio sends from ephemeral port.

conmon_message:0x0080.name: sample_rate_announcement
conmon_message:0x0080.note: Periodic multicast heartbeat (~7.5s interval) on 224.0.0.231:8702 from device port 1027. Contains current_rate at offset 36 and supported rates list starting at offset 48. NOT triggered by sample rate changes — just reflects current state each cycle. After a set_sample_rate (0x0081), the next heartbeat shows the new rate.

history.previous_note: Device multicasts current and all supported sample rates on 224.0.0.231:8702 after a sample rate change.

conmon_message:0x0092.name: reboot_ack
conmon_message:0x0092.note: Device multicast acknowledgment immediately before going offline for reboot.

arc_opcode:0x2010.name: query_tx_channel_names
arc_opcode:0x2010.note: Returns named TX channels. Only channels with custom names are returned.

history.previous_note: Queries TX channel names on port 4440. Protocol 0x2809. Request specifies start channel and count. Response contains channel count at offset 11, then pairs of uint16_be offsets pointing to null-terminated ASCII name strings within the payload.

Queries TX channel names on port 4440 (protocol 0x2809). Request: start_channel at offset 12, count at offset 14. Response: byte 10 = max entries per page, byte 11 = named channel count. Entry table at offset 12: 6-byte entries (channel_num:u16, unknown:u16, name_offset:u16). Names as null-terminated ASCII at referenced offsets. Unnamed channels have no entries. No pagination observed even for 64-channel devices.

Returns named TX channels. Response byte 10=max_per_page, byte 11=named_count. Entry table at offset 12 with 6-byte entries (chan_num:u16, unknown:u16, name_offset:u16). Null-terminated ASCII names follow. Only returns channels with custom names, not all channels.
Returns named TX channels. Only channels with custom names are returned.

arc_opcode:0x2000.name: query_tx_channels
arc_opcode:0x2000.note: Returns TX channel list. Paginated — max_per_page=32. Channel count is rate-dependent: 64 at 1x (48k/44.1k, 2 pages), 32 at 2x (88.2k/96k, 1 page), 16 at 4x (176.4k/192k, 1 page). Paginated responses (status 0x8112) include one lookahead string from the next page. 8-byte records: channel_number(u16), unknown(u16, 0x0107), metadata_ptr(u16), name_ptr(u16). metadata_ptr = 0x0C + channel_count*8, points to 16-byte metadata block with sample_rate(u32) and device-constant fields.
arc_opcode:0x2000.body:
## TX Channel Records (opcode 0x2000)

### Body Header (2 bytes)
| Offset | Length | Type  | Field         |
|--------|--------|-------|---------------|
| 10     | 1      | uint8 | max_per_page  |
| 11     | 1      | uint8 | channel_count |

### Channel Records (8 bytes each, starting at offset 12)
| Offset | Length | Type      | Field        | Notes                              |
|--------|--------|-----------|--------------|------------------------------------|
| 0      | 2      | uint16_be | channel_num  | 1-based channel number             |
| 2      | 2      | uint16_be | unknown      | All channels share same value      |
| 4      | 2      | uint16_be | metadata_ptr | Pointer to 16-byte metadata block  |
| 6      | 2      | uint16_be | name_ptr     | Pointer to null-terminated ASCII   |

### Metadata Block (16 bytes, at metadata_ptr offset)
| Offset | Length | Type      | Field       | Notes              |
|--------|--------|-----------|-------------|--------------------||
| 0      | 4      | uint32_be | sample_rate | e.g. 48000         |
| 4      | 2      | uint16_be | unknown_04  |                    |
| 6      | 2      | uint16_be | unknown_06  | 24 (bit depth?)    |
| 8      | 2      | uint16_be | unknown_08  |                    |
| 10     | 2      | uint16_be | unknown_0A  |                    |
| 12     | 2      | uint16_be | unknown_0C  |                    |
| 14     | 2      | uint16_be | unknown_0E  | 4 (flow count?)    |

### String Area
Null-terminated ASCII channel names follow the metadata block.

history.previous_note: Returns TX channel list with default names. Paginated: request bytes 12-13=start_channel, response includes all channels from start. Response contains channel entries with properties (format, sample rate, etc) followed by null-terminated default names (numeric: 01, 02, etc).
Returns TX channel list with default names. Paginated at 32 channels per page.

Returns TX channel list with default names. Paginated at 32 channels per page. Body header: byte 10=max_per_page, byte 11=channel_count. 8-byte records at offset 12: channel_number(u16), unknown(u16), metadata_ptr(u16), name_ptr(u16). metadata_ptr points to a 16-byte metadata block after all records containing sample_rate(u32) and other fields. name_ptr points to null-terminated ASCII channel name in string area after metadata block.

Returns TX channel list with default names. Paginated at 32 channels per page. Body header: byte 10=max_per_page, byte 11=channel_count. 8-byte records at offset 12: channel_number(u16), unknown(u16), metadata_ptr(u16), name_ptr(u16). metadata_ptr points to a 16-byte metadata block after all records containing sample_rate(u32) and other fields. name_ptr points to null-terminated ASCII channel name in string area after metadata block.

Returns TX channel list with default names. Paginated at 32 channels per page. 8-byte records: channel_number(u16), unknown(u16), metadata_ptr(u16), name_ptr(u16). metadata_ptr points to 16-byte metadata block containing sample_rate(u32) and other fields.

Returns TX channel list with default names. Paginated — max_per_page varies by sample rate (32 at 48k/44.1k, 16 at 192k). 8-byte records: channel_number(u16), unknown(u16, always 0x0107), metadata_ptr(u16), name_ptr(u16). metadata_ptr points to 16-byte metadata block containing sample_rate(u32) and device-constant fields.

protocol_structure:arc_header.name: ARC Protocol Header
protocol_structure:arc_header.note: Common header for all ARC (Audio Routing and Control) messages on port 4440
protocol_structure:arc_header.body:
## ARC Header Format (10 bytes minimum)

| Offset | Length | Type      | Field          |
|--------|--------|-----------|----------------|
| 0      | 2      | uint16_be | protocol_id    |
| 2      | 2      | uint16_be | packet_length  |
| 4      | 2      | uint16_be | transaction_id |
| 6      | 2      | uint16_be | opcode         |
| 8      | 2      | uint16_be | status         |

### Protocol ID
Varies by device model. Known values:
- 0x2729 — LX-DANTE (128ch)
- 0x2809 — AVIO adapters (BT, USB, AES3)

### Status
- 0x0000 in requests
- 0x0001 in successful responses (most opcodes)
- 0x8112 in paginated responses (0x2000, 0x3000)

### Transaction ID
Incremented per request. Response echoes the same transaction_id.

conmon_message:0x0090.name: reboot
conmon_message:0x0090.note: Sends reboot command to device on port 8700. Message type at offset 26.

arc_opcode:0x3010.name: set_rx_subscription
arc_opcode:0x3010.note: Subscribe/unsubscribe RX channels on port 4440. Supports bulk operations up to 16 channels per packet.
arc_opcode:0x3010.body:
## Bulk Subscribe/Unsubscribe RX Channels

Opcode 0x3010 on port 4440 (ARC protocol). Sent to the **receiving** device.

### Header (10 bytes)
| Offset | Length | Type      | Field              |
|--------|--------|-----------|--------------------|
| 0      | 2      | uint16_be | protocol_id        |
| 2      | 2      | uint16_be | packet_length      |
| 4      | 2      | uint16_be | transaction_id     |
| 6      | 2      | uint16_be | opcode (0x3010)    |
| 8      | 2      | uint16_be | status             |

### Payload Prefix (4 bytes)
| Offset | Length | Type  | Field |
|--------|--------|-------|-------|
| 10     | 2      | uint16_be | reserved (0x0002) |
| 12     | 1      | uint8 | count (1-16) |

### Per-Channel Records (6 bytes each)
First record starts at offset 13:
| Offset | Length | Type      | Field              |
|--------|--------|-----------|--------------------|
| +0     | 1      | uint8     | padding (0x00)     |
| +1     | 1      | uint8     | rx_channel_number  |
| +2     | 2      | uint16_be | tx_name_offset     |
| +4     | 2      | uint16_be | src_device_offset  |

Zero-padding follows records to minimum 44 bytes payload.

### String Table
Absolute offsets (from packet start) point to null-terminated ASCII strings:
- TX channel name (e.g. "mic-mix-high")
- Source device name (e.g. "lx-dante")

### Subscribe
Non-zero tx_name_offset and src_device_offset point to string table entries.

### Unsubscribe
Zero tx_name_offset and src_device_offset = clear subscription for that channel.

### Bulk Limits
Maximum 16 channels per packet (string table size constraint). For more channels, send multiple packets.

### Response
10B ACK with status 0x0001 (success).

### Note
CMC opcode 0x3010 on port 8800 is a DIFFERENT command (subscription status polling).

history.previous_note: Subscribe/unsubscribe RX channel on port 4440. Sent to the receiving device.

cmc_opcode:0x3010.name: subscription_status_poll
cmc_opcode:0x3010.note: Subscription status polling on port 8800, sent every ~3 seconds. 70B request/response. Contains receiver device name and metering port (8751). Different from ARC 0x3010 on port 4440.

history.previous_note: DC polls subscription status on port 8800 every ~3 seconds. 70B request/response. Contains receiver device name and metering port (8751). Different from ARC 0x3010 on port 4440.

arc_opcode:0x1003.name: query_device_info
arc_opcode:0x1003.note: Queries device identity on port 4440. Response contains current name at offset 60, factory name at offset 92.

history.previous_note: Queries device identity on port 4440. Response contains current name at offset 60, factory name at offset 92.

disprovals.reason: Name offsets device-dependent: factory_name at offset 92 reads '258' on LX-DANTE (130B response) but correct on AVIO devices (135-137B). Name field boundaries shift with response size.

arc_opcode:0x1001.name: set_device_name
arc_opcode:0x1001.note: Sets device name on port 4440. Name as null-terminated ASCII at offset 10. Max 31 bytes.

arc_opcode:0x1100.name: query_latency_config
arc_opcode:0x1100.note: Queries latency/config on proto 0x2809. On Dante AVIO-USB devices, response byte at offset 0x53 reports the Dante Controller New/configured AES67 state: 0x01=disabled, 0x03=enabled. This does not report the current applied AES67 state.

history.previous_note: Reads device latency configuration on port 4440. 58B request, 140B response. Response contains default, current, max, and min latency values as uint32_be nanoseconds.
Reads device latency configuration on port 4440. 58B request, 140B response. Response contains default, current, max, and min latency values as uint32_be nanoseconds.
Reinstated for auto-disprove test
Reinstated for auto-disprove test

Reinstated after session 58 (latency_discovery_avio). Field offsets 104/108/112/116/120 are IDENTICAL on both LX-DANTE (140B) and all 5 AVIO devices (148B). The 8-byte size difference is in the header/TLV structure, not the latency payload fields. Previous disprovals were based on response size difference, not actual field value mismatch. Tested: AVIOBT-5279b6 (set 5ms/1ms, observed offset 108 change), AVIOAES3, 3x AVIOUSB — all confirmed.

Queries latency/config on proto 0x2809. On Dante AVIO-USB devices, response byte at offset 0x53 reports AES67 mode: 0x01=disabled, 0x03=enabled. Verified in session aes67_toggle via same-device OFF->ON and ON->OFF transitions on avio-usb-1 and avio-usb-3, plus OFF->ON on avio-usb-2.

arc_opcode:0x1101.name: set_latency
arc_opcode:0x1101.note: Sets device latency on port 4440. 40B request, 36B response. Status 0x0001 on success. Proved via read-set-read cycle: sessions 27 (1ms->250us) and 28 (250us->1ms), user confirmed both.

protocol_structure:conmon_header.name: Conmon Protocol Header
protocol_structure:conmon_header.note: Header format for conmon/settings messages on ports 8700/8702. Used for device control (reboot, sample rate, AES67 reconciliation) and multicast announcements. Independent review confirmed this layout, with the 8-byte field at offsets 8-15 treated as an EUI-64 source identifier rather than a 6-byte MAC address.

history.previous_note: Header format for conmon/settings messages on ports 8700/8702. Used for device control (reboot, sample rate) and multicast announcements.
Header format for conmon/settings messages on ports 8700/8702. Used for device control (reboot, sample rate) and multicast announcements.

arc_opcode:0x1000.name: query_channel_count
arc_opcode:0x1000.note: Queries TX/RX channel counts and flow capabilities.

arc_opcode:0x1002.name: query_device_name
arc_opcode:0x1002.note: Queries the device friendly name. Response body is null-terminated ASCII at offset 10.

arc_opcode:0x3000.name: query_rx_channels
arc_opcode:0x3000.note: Queries RX channel list with subscription status. Paginated response. 20-byte records: channel_number(u16), flags(u16), sample_rate_ptr(u16), tx_channel_ptr(u16), tx_device_ptr(u16), rx_channel_ptr(u16), status(u16), subscription_status(u16), padding(u32). sample_rate_ptr points to 16-byte metadata block (same structure as TX 0x2000) containing sample_rate(u32) and unknown fields.

history.previous_note: Queries RX channel list with subscription status. Paginated response.

arc_opcode:0x2013.name: set_tx_channel_name
arc_opcode:0x2013.note: Renames a TX channel. Name is null-terminated ASCII in body.

arc_opcode:0x3001.name: set_rx_channel_name
arc_opcode:0x3001.note: Renames an RX channel. Name is null-terminated ASCII in body.

arc_opcode:0x3014.name: remove_rx_subscription
arc_opcode:0x3014.note: Bulk remove RX channel subscriptions on port 4440. No channel count limit — tested with 17 channels (80B) and 128 channels (524B), both confirmed success. NOTE: DC does not use this opcode for bulk unsubscribe during preset load — it uses 0x3010 with zeroed string offsets instead. 0x3014 may be an alternative removal path.
arc_opcode:0x3014.body:
## Bulk Remove RX Subscriptions

Opcode 0x3014 on port 4440 (ARC protocol). Sent to the **receiving** device.

### Header (10 bytes)
| Offset | Length | Type      | Field              |
|--------|--------|-----------|--------------------|
| 0      | 2      | uint16_be | protocol_id        |
| 2      | 2      | uint16_be | packet_length      |
| 4      | 2      | uint16_be | transaction_id     |
| 6      | 2      | uint16_be | opcode (0x3014)    |
| 8      | 2      | uint16_be | status             |

### Payload
| Offset | Length | Type      | Field              |
|--------|--------|-----------|--------------------|
| 10     | 4      | uint32_be | count              |
| 14     | 4*N    | uint32_be | rx_channel_number (repeated N times) |

### Bulk Limits
No channel count limit — payload is just a count followed by channel numbers (4 bytes each). Tested with 17 channels in a single 80-byte packet.

### Response
10B ACK with status 0x0001 (success).

history.previous_note: Removes an RX channel subscription.
Bulk remove RX channel subscriptions on port 4440. No channel count limit.
Bulk remove RX channel subscriptions on port 4440. No channel count limit — tested with 128 channels in a single 524-byte packet.
Bulk remove RX channel subscriptions on port 4440. No channel count limit — tested with 17 channels (80B) and 128 channels (524B), both confirmed success.

conmon_message:0x0060.name: dante_model_response
conmon_message:0x0060.note: Response containing board codename and board name for device identification.

conmon_message:0x00C0.name: make_model_response
conmon_message:0x00C0.note: Response containing manufacturer, product name, and version. Manufacturer at offset 76 (128 bytes, null-terminated ASCII). Product name at offset 204 (128 bytes). Some devices prepend a 0x01 byte before the manufacturer string — skip non-printable prefix bytes when parsing.

history.previous_note: Response containing manufacturer, product name, and version.

Response containing manufacturer, product name, and version. Manufacturer at offset 76 (128 bytes, null-terminated ASCII). Product name at offset 204 (128 bytes). Some devices (e.g. Ferrofish) prepend a 0x01 byte before the manufacturer string — must use _extract_null_terminated_string to skip non-printable prefix bytes.

cmc_opcode:0x1001.name: cmc_register
cmc_opcode:0x1001.note: Registration handshake with CMC service on port 8800. Includes host MAC address.

conmon_message:0x0BC8.name: identify
conmon_message:0x0BC8.note: Causes the device to blink its LEDs. Fire-and-forget unicast to port 8700.

conmon_message:0x03D7.name: set_encoding
conmon_message:0x03D7.note: Sets device encoding (PCM16/PCM24/PCM32). Unicast to port 8700.

conmon_message:0x0344.name: set_input_gain
conmon_message:0x0344.note: Sets input channel gain level. Unicast to port 8700.

conmon_message:0x0326.name: set_output_gain
conmon_message:0x0326.note: Sets output channel gain level. Unicast to port 8700.

conmon_message:0x22DC.name: set_aes67
conmon_message:0x22DC.note: Enables or disables AES67 mode. Unicast to port 8700.

history.previous_note: Enables or disables AES67 mode. Unicast to port 8700.

disprovals.reason: 0x22DC is the sequence field not the message type. Correct message type is 0x1006. See conmon_message:0x1006.

conmon_message:0x0086.name: config_change_transition
conmon_message:0x0086.note: Multicast notification sent during device configuration changes (e.g. sample rate). Appears ~800ms after set_sample_rate. 40 bytes. Not part of regular heartbeat cycle. Body contains 8 bytes after header: appears to include a timestamp or counter.

conmon_message:0x0082.name: config_change_complete
conmon_message:0x0082.note: Multicast notification sent ~1.6s after set_sample_rate. 60 bytes. Only appears during configuration changes, not in regular heartbeat. May signal that the device has finished applying the new configuration.

arc_opcode:0x1102.name: unknown_1102
arc_opcode:0x1102.note: Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 10B request, 136B response. Polled frequently alongside 0x1100. Possibly extended device settings or latency info.

history.previous_note: Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 10B request, 136B response. Polled frequently alongside 0x1100. Possibly extended device settings or latency info.

arc_opcode:0x2002.name: unknown_2002
arc_opcode:0x2002.note: Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 16B request, 136-156B response. Near 0x2000 (query_tx_channels). Possibly TX channel extended info.

history.previous_note: Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 16B request, 136-156B response. Near 0x2000 (query_tx_channels). Possibly TX channel extended info.

arc_opcode:0x2320.name: set_tx_channel_labels
arc_opcode:0x2320.note: Sent during 'Set Tx Labels' phase of DC preset load. Sets TX channel friendly names on port 4440.

history.previous_note: Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 10B request, 10B response. Minimal payload — possibly a status check or keepalive.
Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 10B request, 10B response. Minimal payload — possibly a status check or keepalive.

arc_opcode:0x2400.name: unknown_2400
arc_opcode:0x2400.note: Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 34B request, 156-180B response. Polled regularly.

history.previous_note: Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 34B request, 156-180B response. Queried regularly by DC.
Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 34B request, 156-180B response. Polled regularly.

arc_opcode:0x2600.name: query_aes67_status
arc_opcode:0x2600.note: Queries AES67 configuration via protocol 0x2809 on ARC port 4440. Response is 131 bytes. AES67 status at byte offset 115: 0x01=enabled, 0x07=disabled. Query packet is 34 bytes fixed.

history.previous_note: Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 34B request, 22-230B variable response. Possibly flow or routing info.
AVIO equivalent of 0x2200 query_tx_flows, used on proto 0x2809. Same semantics but different record format.
Queries AES67 configuration via protocol 0x2809 on ARC port 4440. Response is 131 bytes. AES67 status at byte offset 115: 0x01=enabled, 0x07=disabled. Query packet is 34 bytes fixed.

disprovals.reason: Session 4 contradicts the verified claim that ARC 0x2600 reports AES67 state. Same-device comparisons show non-transaction bytes changing on usb-1 and usb-3 across toggles, but the current user-confirmed enabled device (avio-usb-2) produces the same later value as the disabled devices. The 131B response does not cleanly encode AES67 enabled/disabled.

arc_opcode:0x2601.name: create_tx_flow_2809
arc_opcode:0x2601.note: AVIO equivalent of 0x2201 create_tx_flow, used on proto 0x2809. ~96-104 byte body, response echoes full flow config. Format significantly different from 0x2201.

history.previous_note: Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 106B request, 120B response. Rare (3 occurrences). Near 0x2600 — possibly a set/modify variant.

arc_opcode:0x3300.name: set_rx_channel_labels
arc_opcode:0x3300.note: Sent during 'Set Rx Labels' phase of DC preset load. Sets RX channel friendly names on port 4440.

history.previous_note: Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 10B request, 18B response. Near 0x3000 (query_rx_channels). Possibly RX summary or count.
Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 10B request, 18B response. Near 0x3000 (query_rx_channels). Possibly RX summary or count.

arc_opcode:0x3401.name: unknown_3401
arc_opcode:0x3401.note: Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 38-52B request, 38-52B response. Near 0x3400 (subscription status). Possibly subscription modify via DC protocol.

history.previous_note: Protocol 0x2809 on port 4440. 38-52B request, 38-52B response. Near 0x3400 (subscription status). Possibly subscription modify via DC protocol.

conmon_message:0x0011.name: unknown_0011
conmon_message:0x0011.note: Multicast on 8702. 96-148B variable. High frequency (3165 packets). Possibly device status announcement.

conmon_message:0x0014.name: unknown_0014
conmon_message:0x0014.note: Multicast on 8702. 344B fixed. Rare. Possibly response/announcement paired with 0x0013.

conmon_message:0x0020.name: ptp_clock_status_announcement
conmon_message:0x0020.note: Multicast on 224.0.0.231:8702. PTP clock status announcement paired with 0x0021 config changes. Canonical runtime PTP v1 role field is wire 0x48:0x49 uint16_be with 0x0006=MASTER and 0x0009=SLAVE on devices where v2 is disabled. Wire 0x88:0x89 mirrors the same value on those devices. v2 role detection remains unsupported.

history.previous_note: Multicast on 8702. 168-240B. High frequency (3157 packets). Possibly device capability or config announcement.

Multicast on 224.0.0.231:8702. Paired announcement for PTP clock state/config changes, including preferred-leader-related changes. Runtime Primary v1/v2 role fields remain unresolved; do not treat any single byte as the full leader/follower state yet.

conmon_message:0x0021.name: ptp_clock_config
conmon_message:0x0021.note: Unicast to 8700. PTP clock/preferred-leader config request. Inner payload +0x00:+0x01 is a presence bitmask, +0x02:+0x03 is clock source (uint16_be), and +0x04 / wire 0x24 is preferred leader boolean (0x01=on, 0x00=off). Proven from same-device A32 toggles.

history.previous_note: Unicast to 8700. 92B fixed. Likely request paired with multicast 0x0020.

conmon_message:0x0061.name: unknown_0061
conmon_message:0x0061.note: Unicast to 8700. 32B fixed. Near 0x0060 (dante_model_response). Likely query that triggers 0x0060.

conmon_message:0x0077.name: unknown_0077
conmon_message:0x0077.note: Unicast to 8700. 36B fixed. Paired with multicast 0x0078.

conmon_message:0x0078.name: unknown_0078
conmon_message:0x0078.note: Multicast on 8702. 40B fixed. Likely response/announcement paired with unicast 0x0077.

conmon_message:0x0083.name: unknown_0083
conmon_message:0x0083.note: Unicast to 8700. 40B fixed. Near 0x0082 (config_change_complete). Possibly config query.

conmon_message:0x0084.name: unknown_0084
conmon_message:0x0084.note: Multicast on 8702. 92B fixed. Near 0x0082/0x0083. Config-related announcement.

conmon_message:0x00C1.name: unknown_00C1
conmon_message:0x00C1.note: Unicast to 8700. 32B fixed. Near 0x00C0 (make_model_response). Likely query that triggers 0x00C0.

conmon_message:0x0100.name: unknown_0100
conmon_message:0x0100.note: Multicast on 8702. 40B fixed. Appears during config changes (seen after set_sample_rate).

conmon_message:0x0102.name: unknown_0102
conmon_message:0x0102.note: Multicast on 8702. 34-50B variable. Appears during config changes.

conmon_message:0x0120.name: unknown_0120
conmon_message:0x0120.note: Multicast on 8702. 32B fixed. Appears during config changes (seen after set_sample_rate).

conmon_message:0x01FE.name: metering_data
conmon_message:0x01FE.note: Metering level data. Unicast to a port specified in the metering start command. Default port is 8751 (configurable). 93B fixed payload. High frequency. Triggered by a metering start command that includes the requester's MAC address and desired callback port in the payload. A corresponding stop command exists.

history.previous_note: Port 8751 (metering). 93B fixed. High frequency (7277 packets). Likely metering data.

Metering level data. Unicast to a port specified in the metering start command. Port 8751 is the default used by Dante Controller (configurable — DC complains at startup if the port is unavailable). 93B fixed payload. High frequency. Triggered by a metering start command that includes the requester's MAC address and desired callback port in the payload. A corresponding stop command exists.

conmon_message:0x1006.name: set_aes67
conmon_message:0x1006.note: Conmon AES67 control message sent as fire-and-forget unicast to UDP port 8700. Explicit AVIO-USB writes were observed with trailing words 0x0001 0x0001 (enable) and 0x0001 0x0000 (disable). On LX-DANTE, replaying the observed 0x1006 packet with trailing words 0x0000 0x0000 elicited a fresh 0x1007 Current/New state announcement and appeared to behave as a no-op reconciliation/probe rather than a mutating write. Non-zero sequence number required; sequence 0x0000 was silently rejected.

history.previous_note: Unicast to 8700. 36B fixed. Near 0x1007/0x1008/0x1009 cluster.

Enables or disables AES67 mode. Fire-and-forget unicast to port 8700. Requires reboot to take effect. Trailer: 00 00 00 64 00 01 00 <enable_byte> where enable_byte 0x01=enable, 0x00=disable. Non-zero sequence number required (0x0000 silently rejected). Old fact 0x22DC was wrong — 0x22DC is the sequence field, not the message type.

Conmon AES67 control message sent as fire-and-forget unicast to UDP port 8700. Observed trailer bytes after message_type are 00 00 00 64 00 01 00 <enable_byte> for explicit AVIO-USB writes, where enable_byte 0x01=enable and 0x00=disable. On LX-DANTE, replaying the observed 0x1006 packet with trailer 00 00 00 64 00 00 00 00 elicited a fresh 0x1007 Current/New state announcement and appeared to behave as a no-op reconciliation/probe rather than a mutating write. Non-zero sequence number required; sequence 0x0000 was silently rejected.

conmon_message:0x1007.name: aes67_current_new_state
conmon_message:0x1007.note: Conmon multicast message on 224.0.0.231:8702 whose byte at offset 0x21 encodes combined Dante Controller Current/New AES67 state. Verified values: 0x00=current disabled/new disabled, 0x01=current enabled/new disabled, 0x02=current disabled/new enabled, 0x03=current enabled/new enabled. Verified on AVIO-USB devices for 0x00/0x02/0x03 and on LX-DANTE for 0x01. Fresh 0x1007 announcements can be elicited by sending the observed 0x1006 AES67 reconciliation/control packet to device port 8700.

history.previous_note: Multicast on 8702. 36B fixed. Very high frequency (5407 packets). Possibly heartbeat or status beacon.

Conmon multicast message whose byte at offset 0x21 encodes combined Dante Controller Current/New AES67 state on AVIO-USB devices. Verified values so far: 0x00=current disabled/new disabled, 0x02=current disabled/new enabled, 0x03=current enabled/new enabled. The fourth state current enabled/new disabled is not yet captured.

Conmon multicast message whose byte at offset 0x21 encodes combined Dante Controller Current/New AES67 state on AVIO-USB devices. Verified values: 0x00=current disabled/new disabled, 0x02=current disabled/new enabled, 0x03=current enabled/new enabled. The enabled/disabled state is not expected during normal operation because on->off applies immediately on these devices.

Conmon multicast message whose byte at offset 0x21 encodes combined Dante Controller Current/New AES67 state. Verified values: 0x00=current disabled/new disabled, 0x01=current enabled/new disabled, 0x02=current disabled/new enabled, 0x03=current enabled/new enabled. Verified on AVIO-USB devices for 0x00/0x02/0x03 and on LX-DANTE for 0x01.

conmon_message:0x1008.name: unknown_1008
conmon_message:0x1008.note: Unicast to 8700. 32B fixed. Near 0x1007. Likely query for 0x1007/0x1009.

conmon_message:0x3135.name: unknown_3135
conmon_message:0x3135.note: Port 62976 (non-standard). 317B fixed. Unusual port suggests metering or streaming metadata.

conmon_message:0x40FE.name: metering_data_extended
conmon_message:0x40FE.note: Metering level data (extended format). Same delivery mechanism as 0x01FE — unicast to port specified in metering start command. Default port is 8751. 156B fixed payload. Very high frequency.

history.previous_note: Port 8751 (metering). 156B fixed. Very high frequency (8105 packets). Likely metering level data.

Metering level data (extended format). Same delivery mechanism as 0x01FE — unicast to port specified in metering start command. Port 8751 is the default used by Dante Controller. 156B fixed payload. Very high frequency.

lock_status:0x1102.name: already_in_state
lock_status:0x1102.note: Device already in the requested lock state

lock_operation:0x0001.name: lock_device
lock_operation:0x0001.note: Lock device with PIN. Response lock_state=0x0001 on success.

lock_operation:0x0002.name: unlock_device
lock_operation:0x0002.note: Unlock device with current PIN. Response lock_state=0x0000 on success.

history.previous_note: Challenge request/response. Request is 12-byte message, response is 40-byte message containing 24-byte nonce at offset 0x0010 from message start.
Auth request/response. Request contains operation code at +C and 20-byte crypto_secretbox token at +14. Response contains status at +A and lock_state at +C.

lock_protocol:port.name: udp_port_8002
lock_protocol:port.note: Device lock protocol operates on UDP port 8002. Uses DDP packet framing with 8-byte header, separate from ARC/Settings/CMC services.

lock_protocol:ddp_header.name: ddp_lock_header
lock_protocol:ddp_header.note: 8-byte DDP header: [0:2] total_length=0x0008, [2:4] version=0x0001, [4:6] identifier=0x1000, [6:8] direction (0x0200=request, 0x0300=response).

lock_protocol:handshake.name: challenge_response_handshake
lock_protocol:handshake.note: 4-step handshake: (1) Client sends 0x2FFE challenge request with sequence number. (2) Device responds with 0x2FFE containing 24-byte nonce. (3) Client computes auth token from PIN + nonce using NaCl crypto_secretbox. (4) Client sends 0x2FFF with operation code and 20-byte token. Device responds with 0x2FFF containing status and lock_state.

lock_protocol:crypto.name: xsalsa20_poly1305_auth
lock_protocol:crypto.note: Authentication uses NaCl crypto_secretbox (XSalsa20-Poly1305). Inputs: 32-byte key (ASCII, not hex-decoded), 24-byte nonce from challenge response, 4-byte ASCII PIN as plaintext. Output: 20 bytes (16-byte Poly1305 MAC + 4-byte encrypted PIN).

lock_protocol:challenge_request.name: challenge_request_format
lock_protocol:challenge_request.note: 0x2FFE request: 12-byte message appended to DDP header. [+0:+2] msg_len=0x000C, [+2:+4] opcode=0x2FFE, [+4:+6] 0x0004, [+6:+8] 0x0000, [+8:+A] sequence number, [+A:+C] 0x0000. Total packet is 20 bytes.

lock_protocol:challenge_response.name: challenge_response_format
lock_protocol:challenge_response.note: 0x2FFE response: 40-byte message. [+0:+2] msg_len=0x0028, [+2:+4] opcode=0x2FFE, [+4:+6] 0x0004, [+6:+8] 0x0004, [+8:+A] sequence (echoed), [+A:+C] status, [+C:+E] nonce_offset=0x0010 (relative to msg start), [+E:+10] nonce_length=0x0018 (24), [+10:+28] 24-byte nonce.

lock_protocol:auth_request.name: auth_request_format
lock_protocol:auth_request.note: 0x2FFF request: 40-byte message. [+0:+2] msg_len=0x0028, [+2:+4] opcode=0x2FFF, [+4:+6] 0x0004, [+6:+8] 0x0008, [+8:+A] sequence (same as challenge), [+A:+C] 0x0000, [+C:+E] operation code, [+E:+10] token_len=0x0014, [+10:+12] token_len=0x0014 (repeated), [+12:+14] 0x0000, [+14:+28] 20-byte auth token.

lock_protocol:auth_response.name: auth_response_format
lock_protocol:auth_response.note: 0x2FFF response: 16-byte message. [+0:+2] msg_len=0x0010, [+2:+4] opcode=0x2FFF, [+4:+6] 0x0004, [+6:+8] 0x0004, [+8:+A] sequence (echoed), [+A:+C] status code (0x0000=success, 0x1102=already in state), [+C:+E] lock_state (0x0000=unlocked, 0x0001=locked), [+E:+10] unknown.

lock_protocol:pin_rules.name: pin_format
lock_protocol:pin_rules.note: PIN is exactly 4 ASCII digits (0-9). Sent as plaintext to crypto_secretbox. For lock operation, PIN sets the new lock code. For unlock, PIN must match the current lock code.

lock_protocol:sequence.name: sequence_number
lock_protocol:sequence.note: Sequence number at challenge message +8. Client picks a sequence in the 0x2FFE request. Device echoes it in the 0x2FFE response. Client must reuse the same sequence in the 0x2FFF auth request. Device echoes it again in the 0x2FFF response.

settings_announcement:0x8002.name: lock_status_subblock
settings_announcement:0x8002.note: 0x8002 sub-block in 0xFFFE settings announcement (224.0.0.233:8708) carries device lock state. Offset 16 within sub-block: 0x0001=locked, 0x0002=unlocked. Devices that don't support lock omit this sub-block entirely.

lock_state:0x0001.name: locked
lock_state:0x0001.note: Lock state value 0x0001 in 0x8002 settings announcement sub-block means device is locked

lock_state:0x0002.name: unlocked
lock_state:0x0002.note: Lock state value 0x0002 in 0x8002 settings announcement sub-block means device is unlocked (lock supported but not active)

settings_announcement:0xFFFE.name: settings_announcement_protocol
settings_announcement:0xFFFE.note: Protocol 0xFFFE on 224.0.0.233:8708. Periodic broadcast (~1/sec). Header: 2B proto, 2B length, 4B sequence, 8B EUI-64, 8B magic 'Audinate', 4B version/flags, 4B padding. Body: variable sub-blocks, each with 2B size, 2B sub-opcode (0x8000-0x8004), payload. Sub-opcodes: 0x8000/0x8001 (clock/identity), 0x8002 (lock state), 0x8003/0x8004 (channel config).

arc_opcode:0x2200.name: query_tx_flows
arc_opcode:0x2200.note: Returns all TX flows (unicast + multicast combined), paged at max 16 per response. Header: body[0]=max_flow_slots, body[1]=active_count, followed by pointer table. Record format varies by flow type (unicast=68B, multicast=64-80B). Used on proto 0x2729 and 0x2801.

arc_opcode:0x2201.name: create_tx_flow
arc_opcode:0x2201.note: Creates a multicast TX flow. Body: [0:2]=0x0101, [2:4]=0x0010, [4:6]=replace_slot(0=new), [6:8]=flow_slot, [8:10]=flow_type(0x0002=multicast), [20:22]=channel_count, [22+]=channel_numbers(u16 array). Response: success + assigned slot number. Used on proto 0x2729 and 0x2801.

arc_opcode:0x2202.name: delete_tx_flow
arc_opcode:0x2202.note: Deletes a TX flow by slot number. Body: 00 01 00 00 + u16 flow_slot. Response: empty body on success. Used on proto 0x2729 and 0x2801.

arc_opcode:0x2602.name: delete_tx_flow_2809
arc_opcode:0x2602.note: AVIO equivalent of 0x2202 delete_tx_flow, used on proto 0x2809. 24-byte body, different format from 0x2202.

arc_status:0x0600.name: lock_rejection
arc_status:0x0600.note: ARC response status indicating write was rejected because device is locked. Observed on set_tx_channel_name (0x2013) responses from both avio-bt-1 and avio-usb-1 when device-locked.

history.previous_note: ARC response status indicating write was rejected because device is locked. Observed on set_tx_channel_name (0x2013) responses from both avio-bt-1 and avio-usb-1 when device-locked.
ARC response status indicating write was rejected because device is locked. Observed on set_tx_channel_name (0x2013) responses from both avio-bt-1 and avio-usb-1 when device-locked.
Returns 10-byte empty response

arc_opcode:0x3410.name: subscription_clear_prep
arc_opcode:0x3410.note: Sent to AVIO devices at start of 'Delete Rx Subscriptions' phase during preset load. 28-36B packets. Sent before 0x3010 bulk unsubscribe. Purpose: possibly clears subscription state or prepares device for bulk unsubscribe.

arc_opcode:0x3414.note: Write opcode, blocked by device lock (0x0600)

arc_opcode:0x3610.note: Write opcode, blocked by device lock (0x0600)

arc_opcode:0x3611.note: Write opcode, blocked by device lock (0x0600)

cmc_protocol:magic_number.name: cmc_magic_0x1200
cmc_protocol:magic_number.note: CMC port 8800 uses magic 0x1200, not ARC magic 0x2700. ARC-framed packets get uniform 0x0020 error response.

lock_detection:heartbeat_unreliable.name: heartbeat_lock_state_unreliable
lock_detection:heartbeat_unreliable.note: Heartbeat multicast (224.0.0.233:8708) does NOT reliably indicate lock state. avio-bt-1 shows zero byte differences between locked and unlocked heartbeats. avio-usb-1 reports 0x02 (unlocked) when actually locked. Only avio-aes3-1 may be reliable. Dante Controller uses a different mechanism.

lock_detection:arc_1000_offset_35.name: query_channel_count_lock_field
lock_detection:arc_1000_offset_35.note: ARC opcode 0x1000 response byte 35 (offset 0x23): 0x00=unlocked, 0x04=locked. Works on avio-aes3-1 but NOT on avio-bt-1 (always 0x00 regardless of lock state). Device-dependent — not universal.

lock_detection:latency_probe.name: set_latency_lock_probe
lock_detection:latency_probe.note: ARC opcode 0x1101 with empty 10-byte header: locked devices return 0x0600, unlocked return 0x0001. Works on avio-aes3-1 but NOT on avio-bt-1 (returns 0x0001 even when locked, silently rejects). Device-dependent — not universal.

lock_detection:write_readback.name: write_and_readback_lock_detection
lock_detection:write_readback.note: Only universal lock detection: attempt a write (e.g. set_latency 0x1101), then read back (0x1100) to verify the value changed. If unchanged, device is locked. Works on ALL tested devices including avio-bt-1. Dante devices silently reject writes when locked.

lock_detection:dante_controller_method.name: dante_controller_lock_detection_unknown
lock_detection:dante_controller_method.note: Dante Controller reliably detects lock state on all devices including avio-bt-1 where heartbeat and ARC probes fail. The mechanism is unknown — no non-daemon unicast traffic observed to bt-1. Likely uses DDP protocol (port 8700, magic 0xFFFF) or a field we haven't identified yet.

ddp_notification:0x0102.name: notification_0x0102
ddp_notification:0x0102.note: Most frequent DDP notification from bt-1. 280 instances observed. 35B packets.

ddp_notification:0x0060.name: notification_query_names
ddp_notification:0x0060.note: DDP notification opcode 0x0060. 201 instances observed from bt-1.

ddp_notification:0x00C0.name: notification_make_model
ddp_notification:0x00C0.note: DDP notification containing make/model info. 171 instances from bt-1.

ddp_notification:0x1009.name: notification_1009
ddp_notification:0x1009.note: DDP notification opcode 0x1009. 83 instances from bt-1.

ddp_notification:0x0020.name: notification_0020
ddp_notification:0x0020.note: DDP notification opcode 0x0020. 83 instances from bt-1.

ddp_notification:0x100E.name: notification_settings_status
ddp_notification:0x100E.note: DDP settings status notification. 69 instances from bt-1.

ddp_notification:0x0080.name: notification_0080
ddp_notification:0x0080.note: DDP notification opcode 0x0080. 56 instances from bt-1.

ddp_notification:0x0011.name: notification_0011
ddp_notification:0x0011.note: DDP notification opcode 0x0011. 56 instances from bt-1.

ddp_notification:0x1007.name: notification_1007
ddp_notification:0x1007.note: DDP notification opcode 0x1007. 51 instances from bt-1.

ddp_notification:0x0078.name: notification_0078
ddp_notification:0x0078.note: DDP notification opcode 0x0078. 51 instances from bt-1.

ddp_notification:0x0105.name: notification_0105
ddp_notification:0x0105.note: DDP notification 35B. Body ends 00 01 01. 40 instances from bt-1. Possibly lock-state related change notification.

ddp_notification:0x0084.name: notification_0084
ddp_notification:0x0084.note: DDP notification opcode 0x0084. 30 instances from bt-1.

ddp_notification:0x0104.name: notification_0104
ddp_notification:0x0104.note: DDP notification 35B. Body ends 00 01 02. 28 instances from bt-1. Possibly lock-state related change notification.

ddp_notification:0x0082.name: notification_config_change_complete
ddp_notification:0x0082.note: DDP config change complete notification. 52B. 28 instances from bt-1. Body contains structured data.

ddp_notification:0x010D.name: notification_010D
ddp_notification:0x010D.note: DDP notification 35B. Body ends 00 01 02. Only 4 instances from bt-1, all at 21:44:15 during lock/unlock. Rare — likely lock state change notification.

ddp_notification:0x010C.name: notification_010C
ddp_notification:0x010C.note: DDP notification 35B. Body ends 00 01 03. Only 4 instances from bt-1, all at 21:44:15 during lock/unlock. Rare — likely lock state change notification.

ddp_notification:0x0070.name: notification_board_type
ddp_notification:0x0070.note: DDP notification 68B. Contains board identifier string 'AudinateDIOBT' at offset 0x30. Only 2 instances from bt-1.

settings_opcode:0x0090.name: reboot
settings_opcode:0x0090.note: Reboots the device. Fire-and-forget command sent to port 8700. Device sends multicast ack (0x0092) to 224.0.0.231:8702 before rebooting. Trailer is fixed: 00 00 00 64 00 01 00 00.

settings_opcode:0x0092.name: reboot_ack
settings_opcode:0x0092.note: Multicast acknowledgement sent by device before rebooting. Sent to 224.0.0.231:8702 in response to reboot command (0x0090). Trailer is all zeros.

settings_opcode:aes67_apply_semantics.name: aes67_apply_semantics
settings_opcode:aes67_apply_semantics.note: On Dante AVIO-USB devices, AES67 enable and disable have asymmetric apply behavior. off->on sets New/configured enabled but leaves Current disabled until reboot; on->off applies immediately with Current and New both disabled and no reboot prompt.
settings_opcode:aes67_apply_semantics.body: Confirmed by Dante Controller ground truth during session aes67_toggle. usb-2 showed Current disabled / New enabled after set on without reboot. usb-3 showed disabled / disabled immediately after set off, and the user confirmed Dante Controller does not request a reboot when turning AES67 off. This implies the enabled/disabled pending state is not expected during normal on->off transitions on these devices.

settings_opcode:aes67_current_new_read_p.name: aes67_current_new_read_path
settings_opcode:aes67_current_new_read_p.note: Active read path for AES67 Current/New state: send the observed conmon 0x1006 AES67 reconciliation/control packet to the target device on UDP port 8700, then consume the device multicast 0x1007 announcement on 224.0.0.231:8702 and decode byte 0x21.

settings_opcode:ptp_v2_role_detection.name: ptp_v2_role_detection
settings_opcode:ptp_v2_role_detection.note: Not supported. Session clock_master_control proved preferred-leader config and canonical v1 runtime MASTER/SLAVE read path, but did not isolate a reliable v2 runtime role field.

conmon_opcode:0x0083.name: set_encoding
conmon_opcode:0x0083.note: Set audio encoding (bit depth) on port 8700. Body: [0x1c:0x20]=presence (0x00000064), [0x20:0x24]=set_flag (0x00000001), [0x24:0x28]=encoding_bits (0x00000018=24-bit). Observed during DC preset load 'Set Audio Encoding' phase.

conmon_opcode:0x0013.name: set_interface_address
conmon_opcode:0x0013.note: Set interface IP config on port 8700. Read counterpart is 0x0011 (multicast announcement). Dynamic mode (40B): body is [0x1c:0x20]=presence (0x00000064), [0x20:0x28]=all zeros (DHCP, no IP fields). Static mode (68B): [0x1c:0x20]=presence (0x00000064), [0x20:0x24]=mode_flags (011c0f10 for static), [0x24:0x28]=padding, [0x28:0x2c]=interface_index (0x00000002), [0x2c:0x30]=ipv4_address, [0x30:0x34]=netmask, [0x34:0x38]=dns_server, [0x38:0x3c]=gateway, [0x3c:0x40]=unknown (0x00020000), [0x40:0x44]=padding. All IP fields match preset XML exactly (verified against dc_preset_experiment_2026_03_30.xml).

history.previous_note: Set interface IP addresses on port 8700. 40B for single-NIC devices, 68B for dual-NIC. Observed during DC preset load 'Set Interface Addresses' phase. Sent twice during a preset load (once at start, once near end).

Set interface IP addresses on port 8700. 40B for single-NIC devices (dynamic DHCP), 68B for dual-NIC or static-IP devices. Observed during DC preset load 'Set Interface Addresses' phase. Sent twice during a preset load. The preset XML confirms avio-usb-2 (68B packet) has static IP config while others are dynamic — explaining the size difference. Preset file: tests/fixtures/provenance/dc_preset_experiment_2026_03_30.xml

preset_operation:load_order.name: preset_load_operation_order
preset_operation:load_order.note: DC applies a preset in this fixed order with ~5s gaps: 1) Delete Rx Subscriptions (0x3410+0x3010 zeroed), 2) Clear Preferred Clock Leader (0x0021), 3) Set Interface Addresses (0x0013), 4) Set Preferred Clock Leader (0x0021), 5) Set Sample Rate (0x0081), 6) Set Audio Encoding (0x0083), 7) Set Latency (0x1101), 8) Set Rx/Tx Labels (0x3300+0x2320+0x3010 re-subscribe), 9) Reconciliation (0x3400/0x3401/0x2400). ~46s for 6 devices, ~3000 packets.

conmon_opcode:0x0011.name: interface_status_announcement
conmon_opcode:0x0011.note: Multicast announcement on 224.0.0.231:8702 containing full interface configuration. Broadcast periodically by all devices. Body layout (after 28-byte conmon header): [0x20:0x22]=interface_count (uint16, 1=single NIC, 2=dual NIC), [0x24:0x28]=presence_flags, [0x28:0x2a]=mode (uint16, 0x0001=dynamic/DHCP, 0x0003=static), [0x2a:0x30]=mac_address (6 bytes), [0x30:0x34]=ipv4_address, [0x34:0x38]=netmask, [0x38:0x3c]=dns_or_gateway, [0x3c:0x40]=gateway_or_dns. For dual-NIC devices (lx-dante), secondary interface follows at 0x44 with same layout. Dynamic devices append DHCP domain name as null-terminated string. Packet sizes: 96B (static single-NIC), 108B (dynamic single-NIC with domain), 148B (dynamic dual-NIC).
